query:
-- First, get the user ID and organization ID for 'deji'
-- We'll use a subquery to get these values

-- Insert a test project if it doesn't exist
INSERT INTO projects (name, code, organization_id, status, created_at, updated_at)
SELECT 'Test Invoice Project', 'TEST-INV', organization_id, 'active', NOW(), NOW()
FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%deji%' OR name = 'deji'
LIMIT 1
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE name = name;

-- Get the project ID we just created or that already exists
SET @project_id = (SELECT id FROM projects WHERE code = 'TEST-INV' LIMIT 1);
SET @user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%deji%' OR name = 'deji' LIMIT 1);
SET @org_id = (SELECT organization_id FROM users WHERE id = @user_id LIMIT 1);

-- Insert a completed test session
INSERT INTO sessions (
  project_id,
  title,
  description,
  start_time,
  end_time,
  venue,
  status,
  payment_per_facilitator,
  is_virtual,
  created_by_id,
  organization_id,
  created_at,
  updated_at
) VALUES (
  @project_id,
  'Test Session for Invoice',
  'This is a test session to verify invoice generation works correctly',
  DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 DAY),  -- Yesterday
  DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 DAY) + INTERVAL 1 HOUR,  -- Yesterday + 1 hour
  'Test Venue',
  'completed',
  75.00,
  0,
  @user_id,
  @org_id,
  NOW(),
  NOW()
);

-- Get the session ID we just created
SET @session_id = LAST_INSERT_ID();

-- Add the user as a facilitator for this session
INSERT INTO session_facilitators (session_id, user_id, created_at)
VALUES (@session_id, @user_id, NOW());

-- Record attendance for the user (full 1 hour)
INSERT INTO attendance (
  session_id,
  user_id,
  joined_at,
  left_at,
  duration_minutes,
  was_late,
  calculated_payment,
  organization_id,
  created_at,
  updated_at
) VALUES (
  @session_id,
  @user_id,
  DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 DAY),  -- Joined at session start
  DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 DAY) + INTERVAL 1 HOUR,  -- Left after 1 hour
  60,  -- 60 minutes
  0,  -- Not late
  '75.00',  -- Full payment
  @org_id,
  NOW(),
  NOW()
);

-- Verify the data was created
SELECT 
  s.id as session_id,
  s.title,
  s.status,
  s.payment_per_facilitator,
  a.id as attendance_id,
  a.duration_minutes,
  a.calculated_payment,
  u.name as user_name
FROM sessions s
JOIN attendance a ON s.id = a.session_id
JOIN users u ON a.user_id = u.id
WHERE s.id = @session_id;
